_comment: Hand-maintained regrouping of the build menu tree, layered on top of game_data/generated/buildingCategories.json (the truthful, Docs.json-extracted data) at load time -- never edit buildingCategories.json itself to reorganize the menu, edit this instead. subcategoryOverrides moves an entire internal subcategory (SC_*, see buildingCategories.json/categoryLabels.json) to a different top category in one shot -- every buildable in that subcategory follows it, regardless of which top category Docs.json originally put it under. The target can be an existing internal top category id (e.g. 'Sub_Power') or a brand-new one you invent (like 'Foundation' below); a made-up id needs a display label in topCategoryLabels since it won't be in categoryLabels.json, but overriding onto an existing Sub_* id doesn't (it already has one there).
_comment2: The three class-level sections below cover buildables that are NOT in buildingCategories.json at all (they aren't build-menu entries: they come attached to something else, or their descriptor link is broken in Docs.json) and would otherwise land in the catch-all 'Unknown' category as bare red dots. classCategories values are DISPLAY labels [category, subcategory], not Sub_*/SC_* ids -- these classes bypass the internal id tree entirely (a classCategories entry also wins over buildingCategories.json when the class exists there, e.g. the HUB regrouping below). classFootprintsMeters is [widthMeters, depthMeters] (actor-local X/Y spans) for classes with no usable size data in buildings.json; values were measured from comparable buildables' clearance boxes or the wiki. hiddenClasses (short ClassName) are dropped from the map payload entirely: pure clutter with no informational value (path nodes, sign poles, the pipeline flow indicator, the personnel elevator's moving cabin, and BUILD_Potty_mk1_C -- the toilet actor is only ever spawned BY something the player placed: the HUB's built-in one, the SPWN's attached one, and on old experimental-branch saves glitched stacks of session-respawned duplicates with no surviving parent actor at all. The HUB and SPWN buildings themselves are what the map shows).
